name: High-Voltage AC Transmission
tagline: Extra-high-voltage lines and substations that move gigawatts thousands of kilometres with controlled losses
problem:
A distribution network built for a town cannot push the same power across a continent without turning conductors into toasters. Loss grows with the square of current, so the naive fix — thicker copper — becomes its own logistics problem: every extra tonne of metal needs towers strong enough to hold it and rights-of-way wide enough to keep it from the ground.

Raising voltage lowers current for the same real power, which shrinks both loss and the cross-section you must hang in the sky. That shift buys a new class of headaches: the air itself begins to glow around conductors if the electric field at the surface is too high; lightning and switching surges hunt for the weakest insulator; fog and salt spray turn porcelain into a leakage path.

Long lines are not passive ropes. They store electric and magnetic energy in their length, which shows up as charging current, voltage rise at light load, and sometimes slow oscillations between distant regions. Engineers add reactors, capacitors, and controlled devices as deliberately as steam engineers add valves — not as ornaments but as stability tools.

Without extra-high-voltage corridors, civilisation must sit generation beside load again — good for resilience in one sense, bad for using distant hydro, wind, or nuclear basins. The political shape of energy follows the physics of insulation.
overview:
At a corridor scale, generation voltage is stepped up to hundreds of kilovolts, then carried on bundled conductors that split the electric field across several subwires so corona hisses less. Shield wires ride above phase conductors to intercept lightning; grounding systems give fault currents a controlled path into earth. Substations are fenced cities of steel full of breakers, instrument transformers, and banks of reactive equipment.

Protection is staged like nested fences: relays try to trip only the smallest segment that contains a fault so the rest of the grid keeps serving hospitals and pumps. Operators rehearse N-1 contingencies — losing any single element without losing stability — because weather and backhoes rarely announce appointments.

Environmental engineering matters: corona can sing loudly enough to annoy neighbours; right-of-way clearing changes fire ecology; bird flight diverters reduce collisions. Underground extra-high-voltage appears in dense cities where aesthetics and lightning immunity outweigh cost and heat.

Standards bodies harmonise insulation levels and testing so a breaker from one factory closes against a disconnect from another without a negotiation in the arc gap. That interoperability is easy to forget because it works — until a supplier skips a test and a line trips on mist alone.

principles.name: Surge impedance loading
principles.explanation: Natural power transfer capability of a lossless line relates to voltage and surge impedance — compensation adjusts effective electrical length.

principles.name: Corona onset
principles.explanation: Electric field at conductor surface ionises air; bundle conductors reduce peak field for the same phase voltage.

principles.name: Reactive power flow
principles.explanation: Line inductance consumes vars; shunt reactors at light load prevent overvoltage from line charging.

principles.name: Single-line-to-ground statistics
principles.explanation: Most faults are SLG; zero-sequence networks determine relaying and grounding strategy.

components.name: Bundled phase conductors
components.function: Carries current with controlled field gradient

components.name: EHV substation yard
components.function: Breaks and transforms paths; hosts protection

rawMaterials.name: Aluminium rod for stranding
rawMaterials.purpose: Lightweight conductor with steel core for strength
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Ship ACSR or ACCC conductor from terrestrial mills; early off-world longlines may use aluminium bus in vacuum with wider phase spacing until stranding plants exist; copper where mass matters less than simplicity

rawMaterials.name: Zinc for galvanising
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Hot-dip galvanised structural steel from zinc-rich ore chains; stainless hardware in salt spray without full stainless towers

rawMaterials.name: Porcelain or epoxy insulators
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Composite polymer insulators for weight savings on launch; porcelain where UV and tracking resistance favours mature supply

buildSteps.title: Pick a route humans and wildlife can live beside for decades
buildSteps.description: Walk or fly the corridor before ordering steel. Note crossings of roads, rivers, forests, and houses. Mark where towers must turn, where ice loads worst, and where access roads can reach without carving unstable slopes. Document who owns or governs each segment — a perfect electrical route that ignores land tenure is a lawsuit with foundations.
buildSteps.warningNote: Underestimated icing or wind load collapses spans and kills crews

buildSteps.title: Pour foundations and stand towers plumb
buildSteps.description: Follow geotechnical borings: some soils need micropiles, rock needs anchors. Torque structural bolts to the spec — partial tightening warps lattices. Keep lifting plans conservative; a swinging conductor near an energised line can induce lethal voltages in supposedly dead spans.
buildSteps.warningNote: Work near live lines only under written permits and trained supervision

buildSteps.title: String insulators and set sag with seasons in mind
buildSteps.description: Choose suspension versus tension sets per span physics. Measure sag at the hottest temperature you design for, not only at the pleasant day of construction. Verify ground clearance for vehicles, floods, and crop sprayers.
buildSteps.warningNote: Over-tension cracks porcelain; under-tension touches ground

buildSteps.title: Commission protection before energising at full voltage
buildSteps.description: Test fibre differential or distance schemes end-to-end; verify GPS time sync if your relays use it. Stage through reduced voltage if your procedure allows, watching corona with UV cameras at night. File setting sheets where operators can find them during storms.
buildSteps.warningNote: Wrong CT polarity trips the healthy side and leaves the fault burning

buildSteps.title: Energise, then monitor thermals and noise for a commissioning window
buildSteps.description: Infrared-scan joints after load appears; listen for corona change after rain. Record baseline vibration on sensitive spans. Teach maintenance crews which spans are never to be climbed live and why.
buildSteps.warningNote: Corona ozone and nitric acid can become environmental permit issues near towns

history.event: Three-phase transmission at 25 kV class demonstrates scalable polyphase lines.
First 345 kV lines in North America scale bulk transfer.
HVDC overlays expand for asynchronous interconnection and submarine links.

impact:
EHV turned continents into single electrical machines — with the political entanglements of mutual dependence that implies.

It is prerequisite to siting generation where fuel or sun exists, not where load sits.

videos.title: EHV transmission engineering
videos.description: Accredited power engineering lectures on corona, bundles, and reactive compensation.
